text: Public Feedback: NEET 2024 controversy. Overwhelmingly negative sentiment. Paper leak allegations in Bihar and Gujarat. Grace marks controversy affected 1,563 students. Students demanded re-examination. CBI investigation ordered. NTA reforms announced. Trust in exam system shaken.

Public Feedback: Agnipath scheme. Mixed sentiment. Youth: Protests in UP, Bihar, Rajasthan against 4-year contract. Defence establishment: Supports younger force profile. 46,000 Agniveers recruited in first batch. Retention: 25% after 4 years. Pension savings significant for exchequer.

Public Feedback: Jal Jeevan Mission. Strongly positive. 77% rural households now have tap connections (up from 17% in 2019). Women and children most benefited — reduced water collection time. Some complaints about water quality and non-functional taps in remote areas.

Public Feedback: Women cash transfer schemes. Very positive. Maharashtra Ladki Bahin (₹1,500/month): 2.5 crore applications in first month. West Bengal Lakshmir Bhandar (₹1,000/month): 1.7 crore beneficiaries. Credited for electoral success. Demand for similar schemes nationwide.

Public Feedback: Budget 2025 income tax relief. Highly positive from middle class. No tax up to ₹12 lakh income. Benefiting salaried class and small business owners. Standard deduction ₹75,000. Criticized by some for not addressing high prices and cost of living.

Public Feedback: EVM and electoral integrity. Mixed sentiment. Opposition parties demand return to ballot papers or 100% VVPAT verification. Election Commission maintains EVMs are tamper-proof. Supreme Court upheld EVM usage. Public trust varies by political affiliation.
